question: What pressurizer volume does Table 1.3-1 give for NuScale vs a typical PWR, and how does Table 5.1-1 detail the NuScale pressurizer volume?

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The section describes the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) of the NuScale Power Module, highlighting its role in removing noncondensible gases and maintaining the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary. The RCS, located inside the containment vessel, uses natural circulation to transport heat from the reactor core to the steam generators, with its operation supported by various interfacing systems and components.

nuscale_ch01_0767.title: DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System Malfunction That Increases Reactor Coolant InventoryII.4.B Input Parameters and Initial Conditions - Scram Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0767.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for chemical and volume control system malfunctions that increase reactor coolant inventory, specifically addressing input parameters and initial conditions such as scram characteristics. The NuScale design is shown to conform to the relevant DSRS sections, with clarifications that certain SRP guidance does not apply due to design differences (e.g., use of spring-loaded safety valves instead of power-operated relief valves). Overall, the section documents compliance or explains non-applicability for each regulatory requirement.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses smaller, simpler, and more integrated systems compared to conventional PWRs. Tables provide side-by-side comparisons of key parameters and safety systems for both designs.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, inspection, and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0768.title: DSRS 15.5.1-15.5.2, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System Malfunction That Increases Reactor Coolant InventoryII.4.C Input Parameters and Initial Conditions - Core Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0768.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding malfunctions in the Chemical and Volume Control System that could increase reactor coolant inventory, specifically focusing on input parameters and initial conditions such as core burnup. It states that NuScale’s design conforms to the relevant DSRS sections, and clarifies that certain SRP guidance related to pressurizer relief valves is not applicable because NuScale does not use power-operated relief valves, but instead uses spring-loaded safety valves. Any potential mechanical failure is addressed in a separate analysis section.

edges.description: Section B provides a summary description of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the integral pressurizer, which is the component whose volume is compared in Table 1.3-1 and detailed in Table 5.1-1, thus Section B specifies the technical context for the pressurizer volume values referenced in Section A.
